scout_alien_mk1_master.notes: Baseline Alien Scout unit with balanced claw span, cyan core, and readable biomechanical shell.

scout_alien_elite.notes: Heavier high-rank unit with broader shell armor, stronger core presence, and more threatening claws.

scout_alien_sniper.notes: Elongated precision unit with narrow profile and sharper forward geometry.

scout_alien_swarm.notes: Smaller disposable unit with simplified armor and compact group readability.

readabilityNotes: All units preserve the same hostile biomechanical faction language: bone-green shell, purple chitin, cyan core, and claw-like appendages.
The silhouettes remain separable at 64x64: elite is broad and heavy, sniper is long and narrow, swarm is compact, and mk1 sits between them.
Cyan cores and veins provide recognition markers without overwhelming the shell outline.
Low-noise interiors help the units remain readable behind dense bullet patterns.
androidReadabilityNotes: Recommended display size is 64x64; swarm can be reduced slightly for formations while retaining the cyan core marker.
Outer claw arcs and central shell shape remain legible on small mobile displays.
Avoid scaling below roughly 40x40 for swarm-heavy scenes unless supported by strong contrast or spacing.
silhouetteNotes: Faction identity is defined by insectoid claw span, descending alien body axis, organic armor shell, and a central cyan core.
Elite reads as the highest-threat silhouette due to width and armor mass.
Sniper reads as a precision threat through its thin forward profile.
Swarm reads as a fast disposable unit through a smaller, simpler outline.
integrationNotes: Asset preparation only; no gameplay, runtime logic, collision, hitbox, AI, rank, or balance changes were made.
Sheet frame order is mk1_master, elite, sniper, swarm.
